---
title: "heattree_py"
subtitle: "Interactive Phylogenetic Tree Visualization for Jupyter"
---

::: {.hero}

A Python package for interactive phylogenetic and taxonomic tree visualization 
in Jupyter notebooks using the [heat-tree](https://grunwaldlab.github.io/heat-tree/) 
JavaScript library.

:::

## Installation

```bash
pip install heattree_py
```

## Quick Start

```{python}
from heattree_py import heat_tree

# Minimal example with a Newick string
heat_tree("(A:0.1,B:0.2,(C:0.3,D:0.4):0.5);")
```

## Features

- **Interactive Visualization** - Interactive phylogenetic tree visualization with rectangular and circular layouts
- **Metadata Support** - Visualize categorical and continuous variables through color and size
- **Tree Manipulation** - Collapse, expand, hide, and reveal subtrees interactively
- **Export Options** - Export visualizations to SVG and PNG formats
- **Multiple Tree Formats** - Support for Newick, Nexus, and Python tree objects (ete3, dendropy, Biopython, scikit-bio)
- **Self-Contained** - Works in static HTML exports (no running kernel required)

::: {.callout-note}
## Related Packages

- **[heattree (R)](https://grunwaldlab.github.io/heattree/)** - The R package for R Markdown and Quarto documents
- **[heat-tree (JavaScript)](https://grunwaldlab.github.io/heat-tree/)** - The underlying JavaScript library for web development
:::

## Citation

If you use this package in your research, please cite:

> Foster, Z., & Grunwald, N. (2024). heattree_py: Interactive phylogenetic tree 
> visualization for Jupyter notebooks. 
> https://github.com/grunwaldlab/heattree_py

## License

MIT License. See the [GitHub repository](https://github.com/grunwaldlab/heattree_py) for details.

## Contributing

Contributions and feedback are welcome! Please visit the 
[GitHub repository](https://github.com/grunwaldlab/heattree_py) to report issues 
or submit pull requests.
